data.title: GM Q3 results beat expectations as ICE sales compensate for market share loss in China
data.description:
Fueled by the strength of its internal combustion engine (“ICE”) business which accounts for over 80% of its U.S. business, General Motors (NYSE:GM), General Motors’ (GM) Q3 results beat Wall Street’s expectations with profit and sales exceeding the consensus estimates.

Shares were launched higher in reaction to the results, but have since retraced some of its earlier premarket gains to trade modestly higher into the open.

As the automaker continues to trim costs and consolidate electric vehicle operations, the company’s profit improved to $2.96 per share from $2.28 a year ago beating expectations by $0.56 on a 10.5% increase in revenue to $48.76B, also exceeding expectations.

“I’m proud that GM is delivering our best vehicles ever with strong financial results. But I want to be clear that we are not mistaking progress for winning," CEO Mary Barra said in a letter to investors.

"Competition is fierce, and the regulatory environment will keep getting tougher. That’s why we are focused on optimizing our ICE margins and working to make our EVs profitable on an EBIT basis as quickly as possible,” she added.

North American sales increased by 16% in the latest quarter with trucks and SUVs continuing to make up the bulk of the increased sales. In China, the company’s market share shrunk to 6.5% from 8.3% a year ago, resulting in a loss of $137M versus a profit of $192M in Q3 2023. CFO Paul Jacobson attributes the profitability challenge in China to a “sizable inventory build” that necessitated incentive pricing.

While ICE made up the majority of the company’s U.S. profits, GM (GM) continues to undertake efforts to improve efficiencies and profitability for its EV business.

“EV demand ebbs and flows,” Jacobson said in an interview on CNBC, adding that the company continues to build infrastructure that is flexible, putting the company on a path to achieve profitability. EVs currently make up just 7.6% of GM’s total U.S. market share, with hybrids at 11.1%.

For the remainder of 2024, GM again raised its bottom-line profit forecast to $14B to $15B from the previous forecast of $13B to $15B. Net income forecasts were raised to $10.4B - $11.1B from initial guidance of $10.0B - $11.4B, which should result in an adjusted EPS of $10.00 to $10.50 from the company’s previous forecast of $9.50 to $10.50. Adjusted automotive free cash flow is now expected to be between $12.5B to $13.5B from $9.5B to $11.5B in the company’s previous forecast.

data.title: SAP rises after 'solid' Q3 results and outlook keep analysts bullish
data.description:
SAP's (NYSE:SAP) stock rose about 4% premarket on Tuesday after third quarter results and guidance beat estimates, which drew positive reactions from analysts.

Citi maintained its Buy rating and raised the price target on the stock to €260 from €240, noting that it was another beat and raise quarter, and that the outlook still has potential upside.

The analysts said SAP reported another quarter of solid execution, with earnings for the third quarter and the quantum of 2024 outlook raise coming in well ahead of the elevated expectation set-up. In addition, based on the stellar execution they still see potential upside to both, updated 2024 outlook plus the unchanged (for now) guidance for 2025.

SAP's commentary on the call was confident around business growth and margin levers, progress on innovation (and customer uptake), and go-to-market structure changes from 2025, to support market expansion, penetration, and productivity ambition, the analysts added.

BofA also kept its Buy rating on SAP following a "solid print" and reiterated the company as its Software top pick.

The firm raised the price target on the shares to €243 from €235 ($261 from $252).

The analysts said the third quarter surprised on the upside

SAP's management flagged several drivers that should weigh on backlog growth in the fourth quarter, including a tough 2023 base, leanIX timing, and an uncertain macro. Although the tone was resolutely bullish about the mid to long term growth opportunity, including from AI, a degree of prudence into year-end makes sense in an uncertain macro backdrop, the analysts added.

Meanwhile, JMP maintained its Market Outperform rating on SAP and increased the price target to $300 from $245 after the strong third quarter results.

The analysts said that the cloud growth of 27%, up from 25% last quarter, and current cloud backlog growth of 29%, versus 28% last quarter — all this led to stock up 4% post-market after having surged 48% year to date versus 21% for the Russell 3000 and 23% for the S&P 500.

data.title: IBM to launch IBM Guardium Data Security Center to help protect data
data.description:
• IBM (NYSE:IBM) said it is launching a product to help organizations to protect data in any environment.
  • The product, which is called the IBM Guardium Data Security Center, will provide a common view of organizations' data assets, enabling security teams to integrate workflows and address data monitoring and governance, among other issues in a single dashboard.
  • IBM Guardium Data Security Center also includes generative AI capabilities, which will help in generating risk summaries and boost security professionals' productivity.
  • The product also consists of a software that will help clients to protect encrypted data from the potential risk of future cyberattacks, called IBM Guardium Quantum Safe.
  • "During this transformative time, organizations need to improve their crypto-agility and carefully monitor their AI models, training data, and usage. IBM Guardium Data Security Center – with its AI Security, Quantum Safe, and other integrated capabilities – provides comprehensive risk visibility," said Akiba Saeedi, Vice President, IBM Security Product Management.

data.title: Onfolio acquires majority interest in assets of Eastern Standard
data.description:
• Onfolio (NASDAQ:ONFO) said it has acquired the majority interest in the assets of Eastern Standard.
  • The company bought 70% of Eastern Standard for $1.7M through the issuance of $410K series A preferred shares and two secured promissory notes totaling $1.3M

data.title: Amadeus pares gains after saying it hasn't been contacted by Uber amid speculation
data.description:
Amadeus IT (OTCPK:AMADF) pared some gains in trading in Spain after the company said it hasn't been contacted by Uber (UBER) amid some speculation.

Amadeus (OTCPK:AMADF) has not received any contact from Uber (UBER) and the company has seen increased industry speculation over the recent weeks, according to a Reuters headline on Tuesday.

Shares of Amadeus (OTCPK:AMADF) rose earlier in Spain after a Betaville alert that Uber (UBER) was speculated to be studying a potential acquisition of the company, traders told Seeking Alpha. Betaville also said there's speculation that Uber may also be looking at Grab Holdings (GRAB). Grab rose 3% in premarket trading on Tuesday.

The latest Uber news comes after the Financial Times last Wednesday reported that Uber (UBER) considered a possible takeover of Expedia (EXPE) as it tries to diversify its business.

data.title: Veritone sell media agency Veritone One to Insignia Capital
data.description:
• Veritone (NASDAQ:VERI) said it has sold its media agency, Veritone One, to Insignia Capital.
  • The firm added, the total consideration from the sale will be up to $104M, including $18M in cash dependent on Veritone One’s achievement of certain net revenue targets during calendar year 2025.
  • The net proceeds will be used to retire a significant portion of Veritone’s term debt and to fund future operations.

data.title: Nukkleus to implement 1-for-8 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Nukkleus (NASDAQ:NUKK) said it will implement a 1-for-8 reverse split of its stock on October 24.
  • The firm is implementing the split to regain compliance with the minimum bid price rule.

data.title: Norfolk Southern Non-GAAP EPS of $3.25 beats by $0.14, revenue of $3.1B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Norfolk Southern press release (NYSE:NSC): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.25 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $3.1B (+3.3% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • On an adjusted basis, the operating ratio for third quarter 2024 was 63.4%. This represents 570 basis points of improvement from adjusted third quarter 2023 which was 69.1%.

data.title: PACCAR GAAP EPS of $1.85 beats by $0.03, revenue of $8.24B beats by $650M
data.description:
• PACCAR press release (NASDAQ:PCAR): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.85 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $8.24B (-5.3% Y/Y) beats by $650M.
  • Global truck deliveries of 44,900 units.

data.title: Interactive Brokers Group adds $78M to Q3 expenses for dispute settlement
data.description:
• Automated global electronic broker Interactive Brokers Group (NASDAQ:IBKR) said it will add $78M to its Q3 expenses for settlement of "certain disputes".
  • The stock was trading -0.45% Tuesday pre-market at $148.97.
  • The company reached agreements in principle "to settle certain disputes which relate back to events that took place prior to the end of the quarter".
  • IBKR filed a Form 8-K/A disclosing revised operating results for the quarter ended September 30 yesterday.
  • The company reported Q3 non-GAAP EPS of $1.75, misses by $0.07, and revenue of $1.37B (+20.2% Y/Y), beats by $30M.
  • IBKR has adjusted its Q3 non-GAAP EPS to $1.61 from $1.75. General and administrative expenses are revised to $153M from $75M.
  • Source: Press Release
